prompt: Design a LibOps Offline Collection Orchestrator to help library acquisitions and cataloging teams manage resource collection, metadata quality, and operational risk in a fully offline Android environment. The app serves four roles: Administrator (sets policies, manages roles and secrets), Collection Manager (configures collection rules and schedules, resolves duplicates), Cataloger (maintains taxonomy, holdings, and barcodes), and Auditor (reviews access logs, violations, and reputation signals). Users sign in with local username/password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters with a 5-attempt lockout for 15 minutes and optional on-device biometric unlock after the first successful login. The main experience centers on a “Collection Runs” screen where managers configure entry points (site, ranking list, artist, album) as local sources or imported files, choose incremental vs. full refresh, set priority (1–5), and define retry backoff (1 min, 5 min, 30 min; max 3 retries) with failure isolation so one broken source never blocks others. Catalogers work from a “Master Records” workflow for books/journals/other resources: configurable fields (e.g., Title, Publisher, Pub Date, Format), ISBN-10/ISBN-13 checksum validation, classification taxonomy maintenance, holding-copy counts, and barcode assignment with format rules (8–14 alphanumeric) and collision prevention. Bulk import/export uses local CSV/JSON files with duplicate detection (match by ISBN + title similarity threshold 0.85) and a guided merge UI that preserves an audit trail. Admins can review offline analytics dashboards for funnel metrics (configured → queued → processed → accepted), GMV/order/refund placeholders repurposed as “acquisition spend / items ordered / items returned” totals, plus user/merchant-style reputation scoring adapted to staff quality scores (0–100) based on validation failures and policy violations, with closed-loop alert tracking where every alert must be acknowledged and resolved with a note within 7 days. All data is stored on-device using Room (SQLite) with encrypted at-rest storage for secrets (keys, proxy credentials) and masking for sensitive fields (only last 4 characters visible by default). Core entities include User, Role, Permission, CollectionSource, CrawlRule, Job, JobAttempt, MasterRecord, TaxonomyNode, HoldingCopy, Barcode, AuditEvent, MetricSnapshot, Alert, and AlertResolution; composite indexes (e.g., MasterRecord(isbn13, titleNormalized), Job(status, priority, scheduledAt), AuditEvent(userId, createdAt)) must keep common queries under 50 ms on datasets up to 1,000,000 records. The orchestration engine runs via WorkManager with concurrency control (default max 3 parallel jobs, adjustable to 1–6) and executes heavy IO/computation off the main thread; jobs should pause when battery is under 15% unless the device is charging. UI uses RecyclerView with DiffUtil to maintain 60 fps scrolling for lists up to 5,000 rows, and large cover images (if present in imports) must be downsampled with an LRU cache to keep peak app memory overhead under 20 MB and avoid OOM. Observability is fully local: structured logs with search/filter by time range and correlation ID, exception capture with stack traces, performance metrics (error rate, job latency percentiles, slow Room queries >200 ms), and anomaly alerts (e.g., failure rate >20% over the last 50 attempts) that create actionable tickets inside the app. Any “open API / webhook / SSO” requirements are implemented as offline-compatible file-based integrations (signed import bundles and export bundles) with rate limiting applied to local integration actions (e.g., max 30 imports/hour per user) and strict permission isolation by role.
backend_language: Kotlin (on-device domain/data layers; no remote backend)
